title: The Advice Hour
format: With no movie booked, Sean and Amanda run a live YouTube advice hour, taking listener questions on breakups, weddings, smelly friends, and skincare, with movies dropped in along the way.

films.title: Materialists
films.note: The weekend's flashpoint: everyone in the hosts' lives caught up with it and reported back. Sean splits the reaction into people reflexively defending it because of the cast and people who want the private-equity premise 'sent to Mars.'

films.title: The Phoenician Scheme
films.note: The new Wes Anderson, and the movie a listener took a date to before the date went sideways.

films.title: Past Lives
films.note: One of three breakup movies Luke watched to get over a girl named Amanda. Amanda's verdict: enough wallowing.

films.title: Blue Valentine
films.note: Also on Luke's breakup binge; the pick that gives away the letterboxd boy.

films.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
films.note: The third of Luke's post-breakup watches.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: Sean's comfort watch; he says he fires it up and lets it wash over him like a warm bath, and it has no character named Amanda.

films.title: Amanda
films.note: The Italian film Amanda recommends in general but warns Luke off, for obvious reasons.

films.title: John Wick
films.note: Amanda's throwaway feel-good prescription: just marathon the whole series.

films.title: Bringing Up Baby
films.note: In Amanda's feel-good bucket of classic screwball comedies.

films.title: The Lady Eve
films.note: Another of Amanda's go-to screwballs.

films.title: Pat and Mike
films.note: Amanda's favorite of the Spencer Tracy-Katharine Hepburn pairings.

films.title: Dazed and Confused
films.note: The example in a listener's question about a friend who won't watch movies with 'bad people' in them.

films.title: 28 Years Later
films.note: Sean's Friday plan: drive to Ontario to catch it, then drive back to record.

films.title: 28 Weeks Later
films.note: The middle film Sean just rewatched, mostly to see a young Idris Elba barely used.

films.title: Keeping the Faith
films.note: Cited for young Edward Norton; Sean calls it one of the weirdest movies he saw in theaters.

segments.heading: The Fall-Festival Preview
segments.note: which titles might land at Venice

films.title: After the Hunt
films.note: Luca Guadagnino's next; among the unusual number of US titles slotted for Venice.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: The hosts argue about whether it premieres at Venice; Sean doubts it given the two-week gap to its September 26 release, Amanda wants Leo on the red carpet.

films.title: Caught Stealing
films.note: The Aronofsky movie, which Sean says he doesn't think looks very good.

films.title: Bugonia
films.note: The new Yorgos Lanthimos, which usually premieres at Venice.

films.title: The Smashing Machine
films.note: Benny Safdie's movie with the Rock.

films.title: Father Mother Sister Brother
films.note: Jim Jarmusch's next, floated for the Venice slate.

films.title: Ballad of a Small Player
films.note: Edward Berger's film with Colin Farrell playing poker in Macau.

films.title: Hamnet
films.note: Chloe Zhao's adaptation, which Amanda is stressed about.

films.title: Frankenstein
films.note: Guillermo del Toro's; Sean will watch it, of course.

Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
